_notes: Maps NaPTAN's abbreviated county/region qualifiers (the text NaPTAN puts in parentheses after a station name, e.g. "Hatton (Warks) Rail Station") to the full name Wikipedia's disambiguation convention expects (e.g. "Hatton railway station (Warwickshire)"). Used by scripts/rematch-abbreviation-mismatches.mjs as an extra matching candidate on top of scripts/scope-wikipedia-coverage.mjs's normal candidate list — it does not replace or edit that script's own matching logic. 'observedInStationList' entries were confirmed present by scanning all 62 distinct parenthetical qualifiers across the full 2,637-row station-list.json (2026-07 scoping pass); 'preemptive' entries are the same standard UK county-abbreviation convention (matching Ordnance Survey/postal short forms) but did not happen to appear in that scan — kept here so future re-runs (new stations added to NaPTAN) don't need this table extended for an already-known abbreviation family. Entries already spelled out in full in NaPTAN (e.g. 'Devon', 'Kent', 'Cardiff') and non-county place-name qualifiers (e.g. 'Battersea', 'Glasgow', 'Rhondda') are deliberately NOT here — they need no expansion.
